input.headline: Remote telescope farm helps far away stargazers beat light pollution

raw.headline: Remote telescope farm helps far away stargazers beat light pollution

winner: The remote telescope farm offers "dark sky packages," so you can finally see if that distant star is actually just a misplaced streetlight from your ex's apartment complex.

candidates.text: I tried to stargaze from my apartment, but light pollution was too much—so I invested in a remote telescope farm and now I'm just a guy with a subscription box full of stars.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Funny twist on subscription boxes, specific and surprising with good wordplay."}

candidates.text: I tried using a remote telescope farm to beat light pollution, but all I got was a bad connection and my ex.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.0, "reason": "Puns on 'connection' and 'ex' add surprise and specificity, aligning well with the headline's context."}

candidates.text: I asked the telescope farm if they were tired of the city lights, and they said they were just looking for some space.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, uses wordplay on 'space' (both literal and figurative), and ties back to the headline's context. However, it lacks some surprise and specificity compared to what could be possible."}

candidates.text: I moved my telescope to a remote farm so I could finally see the stars — and also so my neighbors would stop complaining about me staring at their house all night.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, specific, and includes a surprising twist with the neighbor complaint. It ties well to the headline but could be more tightly focused on the 'remote telescope farm' concept."}

candidates.text: The remote telescope farm offers "dark sky packages," so you can finally see if that distant star is actually just a misplaced streetlight from your ex's apartment complex.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist with a specific, relatable scenario that ties the telescope farm to personal life, enhancing humor through unexpected connection."}

candidates.text: I tried to stargaze from my backyard, but the light pollution was so bad, I had to move to a remote telescope farm — now I can see stars, but I'm still stuck looking at my neighbor's porch light.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat relatable and has a mild twist, but the humor is weak and the punchline feels forced. The setup about moving to a remote telescope farm doesn't fully pay off with the neighbor's porch light."}

candidates.text: Since the telescope farm is so far away, I asked the operator if I could see a star and he told me to move back three feet because I was blocking the view of the one in the next county.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat surprising and specific, but the humor is weak due to a lack of clear punchline and some awkward phrasing."}

candidates.text: The remote telescope farm is great for seeing the stars, but it’s a nightmare for the neighbors who just want to enjoy a peaceful night of staring at their own walls.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat surprising and specific, but the humor feels a bit forced and the punchline doesn't fully land due to awkward phrasing."}

candidates.text: Why did the astronomer break up with his girlfriend? Because she said he was always so *light* on our relationship!
